crisis: Drought in ASAL areas of Kenya

tavily.answer: The United Nations’ food agencies have warned that acute hunger is set to worsen across 13 global hotspots, including parts of Kenya, due to conflict, funding shortages, and climate shocks pushing millions closer to famine. In Kenya, a fire at a girls’ school in central Kenya has caused deaths and injuries, highlighting the country’s ongoing challenges. Somalia is also experiencing a deadly drought, with international aid significantly reduced, exacerbating the humanitarian crisis. Famine risks are rising in parts of Somalia for the first time in four years, with severe malnutrition rates among young children. Additionally, Kenya’s private sector activity has been declining for a third consecutive month in May, according to PMI data.
